mitigations.label: bounded-by-spark-rsETH-pool
mitigations.description: When viewed through Kelp as a dependency: a Kelp compromise (LRTOracle price manipulation, rsETH exchange rate fraud) can only affect this function's execution up to Spark's rsETH pool balance. Over-valuing rsETH permits over-borrowing against it up to LTV × rsETH_pool; under-valuing forces unfair liquidation of rsETH positions. Tracked by LRTOracle.sparkRsETHPoolBalance (~15.3 rsETH ≈ $38K).

functions.description: Upgrades the AToken implementation for a given reserve. Internally calls `_upgradeTokenImplementation` → `proxy.upgradeToAndCall(implementation, initParams)` on the AToken proxy. PoolConfigurator can do this because it is the immutable `_admin` of every AToken proxy — set at AToken deployment by `_initTokenWithProxy(new InitializableImmutableAdminUpgradeabilityProxy(address(this)))`. Gated by `onlyPoolAdmin` → `ACLManager.isPoolAdmin(msg.sender)` → POOL_ADMIN_ROLE on ACLManager (currently SubProxy).

Same upgrade path as `updateAToken` but for the StableDebtToken / VariableDebtToken proxies. PoolConfigurator is the immutable `_admin` of those proxies (set at deploy time the same way), and this function calls `proxy.upgradeToAndCall` on them. Gated by `onlyPoolAdmin` → POOL_ADMIN_ROLE on ACLManager.

mitigations.label: rewards-only
mitigations.description: Impact is contained to the protocol's incentives system (wstETH reward emissions). User deposits, withdrawals, and the underlying aToken lending positions are unaffected — the lending-side handleAction hook on ATokens is pointed by POOL_ADMIN, not by EmissionManager. The reward distribution uses a PullRewardsTransferStrategy (0x11aAC1cA..) that pulls wstETH from the Rewards Vault (2/3 GnosisSafe 0x8076...) via transferFrom on each claim. Neither the RewardsController nor the TransferStrategy itself custodies reward tokens. The cap is wired to `EmissionManager.rewardsVaultWstETHBalance` (a live on-chain read of the Safe's wstETH balance), so it updates automatically as the vault is topped up or drained.

mitigations.label: per-user reward theft
mitigations.description: setClaimer authorizes a third party to call claimRewardsOnBehalf(user, to=arbitrary), redirecting the targeted user's unclaimed accrued rewards anywhere. Bounded per call to that user's unclaimed balance, and system-wide to the Vault's live wstETH balance. Deposits, aTokens, and other users' rewards cannot be reached without a separate setClaimer call per victim.

mitigations.description: Grants full control of the reward system (per-reward emissionAdmin or EmissionManager owner). All downstream actions remain contained to the reward system; final drainable value is bounded by the Rewards Vault's live wstETH balance.

mitigations.label: orphan pipe
mitigations.description: Only rewires the EmissionManager's internal pointer to a RewardsController. The ATokens keep their existing IncentivesController (set via POOL_ADMIN on each AToken), so existing accrual continues to be tracked on the old controller and remains fully claimable. Future emission configuration changes via EmissionManager will simply not reach the ATokens — a misconfiguration, not a fund-impact event.

mitigations.label: bounded retroactive
mitigations.description: configureAssets re-checkpoints the distribution using a caller-supplied `totalSupply` (not read on-chain), so a malicious emissionAdmin can retroactively skew accruals for the interval since the last checkpoint. Effect is bounded to emissionPerSecond × time-delta, and ultimately to the Vault's wstETH balance (tracked as EmissionManager.rewardsVaultWstETHBalance). setEmissionPerSecond reads scaledTotalSupply() on-chain and is not exploitable this way.

mitigations.label: claim-DoS
mitigations.description: Replacing the TransferStrategy does not transfer the old strategy's transferFrom allowance, so a malicious replacement cannot pull the Vault's wstETH unless the Safe signs a fresh approve. Worst-case is DoS on future claims (replacement strategy reverts or has zero allowance); already-accrued amounts persist and become claimable once the pipe is restored. Bounded by the Vault's live wstETH balance if a new allowance is granted.

functions.description: Direct entry point to swap the AToken implementation. Gated by `ifAdmin` (msg.sender == _admin), where `_admin` is set immutably at deploy time to the PoolConfigurator address (see PoolConfigurator._initTokenWithProxy → `new InitializableImmutableAdminUpgradeabilityProxy(address(this))`). Because the admin is immutable, no on-chain getter can read it (`admin()` is itself ifAdmin-gated and discovery sees `$admin = ZERO`); the proxy admin is therefore exposed via the hardcoded `proxyAdmin` field set in the spark/AToken template. In practice this function is only ever called from PoolConfigurator.updateAToken / .updateStableDebtToken / .updateVariableDebtToken, all gated by POOL_ADMIN_ROLE on ACLManager.

Because the admin is immutable, no on-chain getter can read it (`admin()` is itself ifAdmin-gated and discovery sees `$admin = ZERO`); the proxy admin is therefore exposed via the hardcoded `proxyAdmin` field set in the spark/AToken template. Burning on liquidation (Pool.executeLiquidationCall → _burnCollateralATokens) uses WSTETHExchangeRateOracle via AaveOracle.getAssetPrice to compute how much collateral to seize. Burning on withdrawal (Pool.executeWithdraw) is gated by validateHFAndLtv, which consults the same oracle post-burn to revert if the remaining position would be unhealthy. If this oracle misreports the price of spwstETH, liquidators seize the wrong amount of this AToken and underwater accounts can drain the reserve via withdraw, exposing depositors to bad debt. If WSTETHExchangeRateOracle reverts, AaveOracle falls back to `getFallbackOracle` (currently address(0) — effectively no fallback).

Called only from Pool.executeLiquidationCall — transfers collateral aTokens from the liquidated user to the liquidator (when `receiveAToken=true`) or to the treasury (liquidation protocol fee). The transferred amount is derived from the collateral/debt prices reported by WSTETHExchangeRateOracle via AaveOracle.getAssetPrice, so a wrong price mis-sizes the liquidator's reward and the victim's loss. If WSTETHExchangeRateOracle reverts, AaveOracle falls back to `getFallbackOracle` (currently address(0) — effectively no fallback).

Because the admin is immutable, no on-chain getter can read it (`admin()` is itself ifAdmin-gated and discovery sees `$admin = ZERO`); the proxy admin is therefore exposed via the hardcoded `proxyAdmin` field set in the spark/AToken template. In practice this function is only ever called from PoolConfigurator.updateAToken / .updateStableDebtToken / .updateVariableDebtToken, all gated by POOL_ADMIN_ROLE on ACLManager.

Burning on liquidation (Pool.executeLiquidationCall → _burnCollateralATokens) uses WEETHExchangeRateOracle via AaveOracle.getAssetPrice to compute how much collateral to seize. Direct entry point to swap the AToken implementation. Gated by `ifAdmin` (msg.sender == _admin), where `_admin` is set immutably at deploy time to the PoolConfigurator address (see PoolConfigurator._initTokenWithProxy → `new InitializableImmutableAdminUpgradeabilityProxy(address(this))`). Because the admin is immutable, no on-chain getter can read it (`admin()` is itself ifAdmin-gated and discovery sees `$admin = ZERO`); the proxy admin is therefore exposed via the hardcoded `proxyAdmin` field set in the spark/AToken template. In practice this function is only ever called from PoolConfigurator.updateAToken / .updateStableDebtToken / .updateVariableDebtToken, all gated by POOL_ADMIN_ROLE on ACLManager.

functions.description: DSAuth pattern: gated by `auth` modifier checking msg.sender == owner OR authority.canCall(...). DSPause has owner=ZERO so the only path is via the `authority` contract (DSChief governance — Maker-style on-chain voting).

Chronicle Protocol's Auth pattern (NOT DSAuth — separate impl). Gated by `auth` modifier checking _wards[msg.sender] == 1. The `_wards` mapping is private with no zero-arg getter, so discovery can't enumerate the authorized addresses (the parameterized `wards(address)` view exists but isn't callable without args). Chronicle Protocol controls who can rely/deny — this oracle is shared infra, not Spark-governed.

Chronicle Protocol's Toll pattern. Gated by `toll_auth` (= same `auth` modifier as rely/deny). kiss/diss whitelist/un-whitelist addresses that can READ the price (latestAnswer/latestRoundData are view+toll). Same Chronicle governance applies.

mitigations.label: bounded-by-spark-rsETH-pool
mitigations.description: Any Kelp-side compromise (LRTOracle price manipulation, rsETH exchange rate fraud) can only affect Spark up to the rsETH collateral held by the Spark lending pool. Tracked by LRTOracle.sparkRsETHPoolBalance (live on-chain balance of Spark's rsETH AToken).

mitigations.label: bounded-by-spark-rsETH-pool
mitigations.description: Any Kelp-side compromise (LRTOracle price manipulation, rsETH exchange rate fraud) can only affect Spark up to the rsETH collateral held by the Spark lending pool. Over-valuing rsETH lets attackers over-borrow against it (drain other assets up to rsETH-collateral × LTV); under-valuing unfairly liquidates rsETH-collateralized positions. Both paths are bounded by Spark's rsETH pool balance (tracked as LRTOracle.sparkRsETHPoolBalance — currently 15.32 rsETH ≈ $38K).
